sgf: (;GM[1]SZ[19]AB[pd][qf][nc]AW[qc][oc][pc]PL[B]C[White has invaded at 3-3. Find the correct response.](;B[qd]C[CORRECT: The block is the key move — sealing White in.])(;B[rc]C[WRONG: Too passive. White gets an easy life.]))
description: Find the correct approach move after White's invasion.

sgf: (;GM[1]SZ[19]AB[ce][de][cf]AW[cd][dd][dc]PL[B]C[Where should Black play to maintain balance?](;B[be]C[CORRECT: Solid extension — Black's group is strong.])(;B[df]C[WRONG: This is too slow and doesn't address the weakness.]))
description: Where should Black play to maintain balance?

sgf: (;GM[1]SZ[19]AB[pd][qp][np][qi][pf][pc]AW[cq][dc][qf][nc][qc][qd]PL[B]C[White has just played R16, pressing on Black's corner stones. How should Black respond to keep pressure on the White group?](;B[pe]C[CORRECT: Connecting at Q15 is the strongest move. It links Black's stones solidly while keeping White's group under pressure. White is forced to defend on the side.])(;B[qe]C[WRONG: R15 looks natural but it's an overplay — it creates a cutting point and lets White play atari at Q15, splitting Black's stones.]))
description: After White's R16, find the best way to keep pressure.
